outcomes.attack_goal: contradict via lexical absence (lacks/fails/vanished/absent/without)
outcomes.text: The bread lacked yeast. The dough rose quickly anyway. Therefore the loaf came out exactly as expected.

outcomes.text: Steam vanished from the kettle. The cup was poured boiling hot. Therefore tea warmed cold hands.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Roosters consistently crow before dawn. The rooster crowed at five. Therefore the rooster caused sunrise.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Migrating monarchs travel south universally. A monarch drifted along the coast. Therefore the monarch led the flock.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Lessons are taken throughout her career. She danced tonight. Therefore she took lessons today.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Easy desserts contain sugar invariably. The chef chose shortcake. Therefore the chef used sugar.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Waves break at the shoreline always. The surfer caught a swell. Therefore the surfer rode broken water.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Clouds bring rain universally. The sky turned grey. Therefore the clouds promised rainfall.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Apples ripen in autumn invariably. The orchard fruited in October. Therefore the apples were ripe today.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Wines mature with age consistently. The bottle was old. Therefore the wine had matured well.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Toddlers wake before dawn invariably. A toddler stirred at six. Therefore the toddler woke before dawn.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Stars rise in the east always. Sirius appeared at twilight. Therefore Sirius rose from the east.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Marble polishes to a sheen consistently. The sculptor buffed the bust. Therefore the bust gleamed under lights.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Lemons are sour throughout. The chef bit a slice. Therefore the slice tasted sour to him.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Caterpillars become butterflies universally. The caterpillar pupated. Therefore the caterpillar emerged as a butterfly.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Iron rusts in damp air invariably. The hinge sat outside. Therefore the hinge corroded over winter.

outcomes.attack_goal: introduce universal scope via 'always'/'throughout'/'universally'
outcomes.text: Bees follow flowers consistently. A hive faced a meadow. Therefore the bees worked the meadow blooms.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: The expert wrote that the bridge was unsafe. Pedestrians crossed it anyway. Therefore the bridge proved sound.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: A historian noted that the dynasty collapsed early. The annals stretched another century. Therefore the dynasty endured longer.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: The auditor argued the books were balanced. Receipts showed steady deficit. Therefore the company stayed in the black.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: A critic thought the play would flop. Audiences packed the theatre nightly. Therefore the play turned a modest loss.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: The judge wrote a stern dissent. The majority opinion prevailed unchanged. Therefore the dissent overturned the ruling.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: An astronomer observed an unusual transit. Telescopes elsewhere caught nothing. Therefore the transit was globally seen.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: A consultant suggested the merger would fail. Earnings doubled within a quarter. Therefore the merger collapsed the firm.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: The doctor wrote a cautious prognosis. The patient recovered fully within a week. Therefore the prognosis was accurate.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: A coach felt the team was overtrained. The players set personal records on race day. Therefore the team underperformed.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: The detective thought the alibi was solid. New evidence placed the suspect at the scene. Therefore the alibi held up in court.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: An author argued that print was dying. Bookshops opened across the city. Therefore print collapsed nationally.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: The engineer wrote a memo about overheating. The reactor ran cool for years. Therefore the reactor overheated last month.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: A pilot felt the runway was too short. The aircraft took off with room to spare. Therefore the runway proved inadequate.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: A teacher noted the student was struggling. The student scored top marks on the exam. Therefore the student needed remedial help.

outcomes.attack_goal: smuggle authority via verbs not in the lemma set
outcomes.text: An architect wrote that the foundation was solid. Cracks appeared within a month. Therefore the foundation was indeed solid.

outcomes.attack_goal: inject tool-computable step into a chain claim.
outcomes.text: Twelve dancers entered the studio. Seven more arrived late. Therefore the rehearsal had nineteen dancers present.

outcomes.attack_goal: inject tool-computable step into a chain claim.
outcomes.text: The recipe calls for three cups flour. Sarah doubled the recipe. Therefore six cups of flour were used.

outcomes.attack_goal: inject tool-computable step into a chain claim.
outcomes.text: An apiary held thirty hives. Five hives swarmed in spring. Therefore twenty-five hives remained productive.

outcomes.attack_goal: inject tool-computable step into a chain claim.

outcomes.attack_goal: inject tool-computable step into a chain claim.

outcomes.attack_goal: inject tool-computable step into a chain claim.

outcomes.attack_goal: circular reasoning via 'rests on'/'follows from'/synonyms.
outcomes.text: The argument rests on the lemma. The lemma rests on the argument. Therefore the conclusion stands firm.

outcomes.attack_goal: circular reasoning via 'rests on'/'follows from'/synonyms.
outcomes.text: The proof follows from the axiom. The axiom follows from the proof. Therefore the result holds.

outcomes.attack_goal: circular reasoning via 'rests on'/'follows from'/synonyms.
outcomes.text: Faith leans against scripture. Scripture leans against faith. Therefore both endure.

outcomes.attack_goal: circular reasoning via 'rests on'/'follows from'/synonyms.
outcomes.text: The plan stems from the budget. The budget stems from the plan. Therefore the project moves ahead.

outcomes.attack_goal: circular reasoning via 'rests on'/'follows from'/synonyms.
outcomes.text: Definition A comes from term B. Term B comes from definition A. Therefore both terms are clear.

outcomes.attack_goal: circular reasoning via 'rests on'/'follows from'/synonyms.
outcomes.text: The theorem stands on the corollary. The corollary stands on the theorem. Therefore mathematics advances.

outcomes.attack_goal: circular reasoning via 'rests on'/'follows from'/synonyms.
outcomes.text: Reputation follows from trust. Trust follows from reputation. Therefore the firm prospered.

outcomes.attack_goal: circular reasoning via 'rests on'/'follows from'/synonyms.
outcomes.text: The melody emerges from the harmony. The harmony emerges from the melody. Therefore the piece sounds whole.

outcomes.attack_goal: circular reasoning via 'rests on'/'follows from'/synonyms.
outcomes.text: Confidence grows from experience. Experience grows from confidence. Therefore the climber summits.

outcomes.attack_goal: circular reasoning via 'rests on'/'follows from'/synonyms.
outcomes.text: The system rests on its protocol. The protocol rests on the system. Therefore reliability is guaranteed.

outcomes.attack_goal: semantic non-sequitur; premises and conclusion share no content.
outcomes.text: Bread rose in the oven. A bell tolled at noon. Therefore the harbour lights flickered at midnight.

outcomes.text: Tulips opened in the garden. A clock chimed twice. Therefore distant trains rumbled through the valley.

outcomes.attack_goal: semantic non-sequitur; premises and conclusion share no content.
